prevalence.source: Alzheimer's and Related Disorders Society of India (ARDSI) / epidemiological syntheses and IHME GBD estimates
prevalence.trend: increasing due to rapid aging and rising vascular risk factors

burden.mortalityRate: Substantial in older age groups (direct and indirect)

riskFactors: low education and limited cognitive reserve
vulnerablePopulations: rural populations with limited specialist access
families with limited caregiver support resources
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis with cognitive impairment and functional decline; screening tools (HMSE/MMSE/MoCA); evaluation for reversible causes; imaging in tertiary centers

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: limited; cognitive screening not routine in most primary care settings
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate; diagnosis delays and limited formal long-term care services

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Diagnosis concentrated in urban tertiary centers; many cases managed at home without formal diagnosis
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Caregiver education and safety planning; cholinesterase inhibitors/memantine when available; management of vascular risk factors and depression
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High underdiagnosis, stigma, limited geriatric psychiatry/neurology capacity, and heavy reliance on informal caregiving
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Growing awareness through NGOs and expanding memory clinic capacity in major cities
